explanation:
Paid vacation is a fundamental labor right in Argentina protected by law. This calculator determines your earned vacation days based on tenure under the Labor Contract Law (LCT Article 150).

## How it works

Your vacation entitlement depends on years of service with your employer. The LCT sets mandatory minimums that all employers must provide.

## Vacation day scale by tenure

| Years of Service | Vacation Days |
|---|---|
| Less than 5 years | 14 days |
| 5-10 years | 21 days |
| 10-20 years | 28 days |
| 20+ years | 35 days |

## Key vacation rights

- **Non-waivable right**: You cannot give up or forfeit your vacation time.
- **Paid time**: You receive your full salary during vacation.
- **Tenure-based**: Entitlements increase with continuous service.
- **Accumulation allowed**: Up to 50% of annual days can carry to the next year.
- **Scheduling flexibility**: Standard period is October-April, but dates can be negotiated.

## Important notes

This calculation is for reference only. For specific employment situations, consult an HR professional or labor attorney. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.q: What law governs vacation days in Argentina?
faq.a: Argentina's Labor Contract Law (LCT) 20.744, Articles 150-157, establishes mandatory vacation day minimums.

faq.q: What's the vacation day scale by years of service?
faq.a: Up to 5 years: 14 days; 5-10 years: 21 days; 10-20 years: 28 days; 20+ years: 35 days.

faq.q: How are vacation days calculated in the first year?
faq.a: If you haven't completed 6 months of employment, you earn 1 day per month worked (pro-rata basis).

faq.q: Are vacation days calendar days or working days?
faq.a: They are calendar days, including weekends and holidays.

faq.q: When can I take my vacation days?
faq.a: The standard vacation period is October-April, but employers and employees can negotiate alternative dates.

faq.q: Can I sell or waive my unused vacation days?
faq.a: No. Vacation time is a non-waivable right under Argentine law.

faq.q: Can I accumulate vacation days to the next year?
faq.a: Yes, you can accumulate up to 50% of your annual entitlement to the following year.

faq.q: What if I leave my job? Do I get paid for unused vacation?
faq.a: Yes. Unused vacation days must be paid out at your regular salary rate when employment ends.

faq.q: How is vacation calculated for part-time employees?
faq.a: Part-time workers receive proportional vacation days based on their hours worked.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, does not require external data updates
